# Formal verification of the go-crm design

Every model here is checked exhaustively by TLC. Nothing is asserted by inspection.
Run the whole suite from the repo root:

```sh
make verify-formal        # regenerates everything from source, runs all 8 proofs
```

Requires Java 11+. `tlc.sh` fetches `tla2tools.jar` into `~/.cache/machinery` on first use.
Everything is regenerated from source on each run, so the proofs cannot drift from the design.

## 1. Control-flow models (generated by `tla_gen`)

`Deal.tla`, `Task.tla`, `User.tla`, `Session.tla`, `CommandExecution.tla` are generated from
the machine JSON. The state graph is exact; guards are over-approximated, which is sound for the
properties below because they hold over a superset of the real behavior. The persist-retry overlay
is modeled with a bounded counter so liveness is decidable. Each proves:

- `TypeOK` (safety): valid states, retry counter never exceeds its bound.
- `Live_OverlayResolves` (liveness): any transient overlay state always returns to a resting domain
  state. No stuck half-persist, no infinite retry.
- deadlock-freedom (final states stutter via `Terminated`).

State counts: Deal 18, Task 16, User 14, Session 24, CommandExecution 31.

## 2. Data-refined domain invariants (generated by `refine_gen` from `*.semantics.yaml`)

The control-flow model abstracts away the committed data. `refine_gen.py` reads the machine plus a
short declarative `Deal.semantics.yaml` (the meaning the JSON omits: stage order, terminals, reopen,
close-date) and generates `DealData.tla`, which proves the real domain contract over 200 states:

- `Inv_Atomic`: persist atomicity. The committed stage flips only on the atomic commit; any failure
  rolls back to the prior stage unchanged. No torn state is reachable.
- `StageForward`: the `deal-stage-forward` invariant (forward, to Won/Lost, or reopen only).
- `Inv_CloseDate`: the `deal-won-has-closedate` invariant. Plus well-formedness and liveness.

Because it is generated from an eight-line annotation, a new lifecycle aggregate is a short spec,
not a hand-written proof.

## 3. Refinement (generated: `DealContract` + `DealRefinement`)

`DealContract.tla` is the abstract contract the big picture assumes of a deal aggregate: resting or
busy, atomic while busy, always terminating. `DealRefinement.tla` defines the mapping from the
concrete `DealData` state to the contract and TLC proves every concrete behavior is a contract
behavior (`RefSpec`, `RefTermination`). A subsystem is a correct part of the whole exactly when it
refines the contract its neighbors assumed.

## 4. Assume-guarantee composition (`System`)

`System.tla` composes a caller (the command envelope) with the deal aggregate known ONLY through
`DealContract`. It proves the caller's guarantee: a running command always completes
(`Live_CommandCompletes`) and leaves the deal resting (`Safe_DoneImpliesResting`), assuming only the
contract. Because `DealData` refines `DealContract` (section 3), the real system inherits these
guarantees, so the parts are verified against small contracts and never against the flattened whole.
That is the rule that lets the approach scale to large systems.
